articles.title: E8 Properties Converting Vacant Retail Into Family Entertainment Centers
articles.summary: Real estate firm E8 Properties is repurposing shuttered retail spaces into family entertainment centers, a trend gaining momentum as landlords seek experiential tenants to fill anchor vacancies. This signals continued opportunity for FEC operators looking to negotiate favorable lease terms in former big-box or mall locations. Operators and developers should watch this adaptive reuse model as a blueprint for expansion.

articles.title: $3 Billion Motown Sports Village Planned Near Detroit with Arena, Water Park, IMAX, and More
articles.summary: A massive $3 billion mixed-use entertainment destination is planned near Detroit Metro Airport, incorporating an arena, water park, IMAX theater, hotels, and additional attractions. Projects of this scale represent both competitive pressure and partnership opportunity for regional FEC operators. The development underscores the continued investor appetite for large-format, experience-driven entertainment destinations.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market Sees Strong Investment and Growth Trends
articles.summary: A new market report highlights rising investment activity and favorable growth trends in the location-based entertainment sector. For FEC operators and investors, this reinforces the sector's appeal as a destination for capital allocation. The report likely covers arcade, VR, and experiential entertainment venues that overlap directly with FEC business models.

articles.title: UK Slashes VAT to 5% on Theme Parks, Museums, and Attractions This Summer
articles.summary: The UK government is cutting VAT from 20% to 5% on eligible attractions including theme parks, museums, and zoos from June 25 to September 1 as part of a 'Great British Summer Savings' scheme. The temporary reduction is designed to ease cost-of-living pressure on families while boosting attendance at leisure venues. UK-based FEC operators should plan marketing campaigns and pricing strategies around this window to maximize footfall.

articles.title: OpenAire to Build Retractable Roof Enclosures for Mattel Wonder Indoor Waterparks
articles.summary: OpenAire has partnered with American Resort Management and Martin Aquatic to develop all-weather retractable roof enclosures for a series of Mattel-branded indoor waterparks featuring Barbie and Hot Wheels themes. The partnership highlights the growing demand for all-season aquatic attractions that reduce weather dependency. FEC developers and operators eyeing indoor waterpark concepts should note this project as a reference model for scalable, weather-proof design.

articles.title: Cinemark's Gamescape FEC Concept Cancelled at Merriam Town Center
articles.summary: Cinemark's planned Gamescape entertainment venue at Merriam Town Center in Kansas has been scrapped, marking a setback for the theater chain's push into the FEC space. The cancellation raises questions about the viability of cinema-adjacent entertainment concepts in secondary markets. Competing FEC operators in the area may find this creates an opening, while industry watchers should monitor whether Cinemark abandons the format or simply relocates.

articles.title: Peter Piper Pizza Unveils Reimagined Concept Location in Texas
articles.summary: Peter Piper Pizza is opening a redesigned experience in Texas, signaling a brand refresh for the longtime family dining and entertainment chain. The reimagined concept likely incorporates updated games, food offerings, and guest experience enhancements in line with modern FEC standards. Operators competing in the family dining-plus-entertainment segment should take note of how legacy brands are evolving to stay relevant.

articles.title: Mattel Wonder Indoor Waterpark Announced for Planned Weldon Mills Resort
articles.summary: A Mattel Wonder indoor waterpark has been confirmed as part of the planned Weldon Mills Resort development, expanding the branded waterpark concept's footprint. The project is part of a broader rollout of Mattel IP-licensed indoor aquatic attractions. FEC and waterpark developers should monitor this IP licensing model as a potential differentiator for new venue concepts.

articles.title: Supercharged Entertainment Concept Eyed for Polaris District Alongside Topgolf and Smash Park
articles.summary: A Supercharged entertainment venue is being considered for the Polaris mixed-use district in Ohio, joining existing tenants Topgolf and Smash Park in what is becoming a concentrated entertainment corridor. The clustering of experiential entertainment venues in a single district demonstrates the viability of entertainment-anchored retail destinations. FEC operators should consider how proximity to complementary venues can drive cross-traffic and shared marketing opportunities.

articles.title: Expert Guide to Designing Accessible Attractions: From Compliance to Inclusion
articles.summary: Industry experts outline best practices for building accessibility into attraction design from the concept stage, emphasizing that 1.2 billion people globally have additional needs and represent a major market opportunity. Key takeaways include eliminating separate entrances, baking accessibility into RFPs, and prioritizing predictability for neurodivergent guests. FEC designers and operators are urged to consult people with lived experience early rather than retrofitting solutions later.

articles.title: Tenpin Bowling and Entertainment Centre Opens in Blackpool This June
articles.summary: A new Tenpin entertainment centre in Blackpool is now accepting bookings ahead of its June opening, adding bowling and entertainment options to a market already known for leisure tourism. The opening reflects Tenpin's continued UK expansion strategy combining bowling with broader entertainment offerings. FEC operators can look to Tenpin's location selection and product mix as a benchmark for entertainment center development in tourist-heavy markets.

articles.title: $2M Remodeling Permit Approved for Former AMC Theater in Marion, Illinois
articles.summary: A former AMC movie theater in Marion, Illinois has received a $2 million remodeling permit, suggesting a conversion to a new use — potentially an entertainment venue. Theater-to-FEC conversions are an emerging trend as cinema attendance softens and landlords seek experiential replacements. FEC operators and developers should monitor such adaptive reuse projects as cost-effective entry points into established retail or entertainment locations.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Center Opens in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: Thunderzone Family Entertainment has opened a new location in Carrollton, Georgia, bringing multi-attraction FEC experiences to the local market. New FEC openings in smaller markets signal continued confidence in the sector's growth beyond major metro areas. Operators and suppliers can track this opening as an example of FEC expansion into secondary and tertiary markets.

articles.title: Slick City Indoor Slide Park Opening New Location in Milford This Summer
articles.summary: Slick City, the indoor slide and entertainment park concept, is set to open a new location in Milford this summer, continuing the brand's national expansion. The concept targets families and groups with its unique slide-based attraction format, competing in the active entertainment space alongside trampoline parks and ninja courses. FEC operators should watch Slick City's growth as it adds to the competitive landscape for active family entertainment.

articles.title: Rising Heat and Pollution Driving Boom in Indoor Play and Entertainment
articles.summary: An analysis highlights how increasing temperatures and outdoor air quality concerns are accelerating demand for indoor entertainment options, particularly in urban and suburban markets. This macro trend is a tailwind for FEC operators offering climate-controlled experiences. Operators can leverage this insight in marketing and site-selection strategies to capitalize on weather-driven guest migration to indoor venues.

articles.title: Interactive Sports and Gaming Venue from London to Open in Paris
articles.summary: A London-based indoor entertainment concept featuring virtual soccer, interactive games, foosball, cocktails, and fast food is expanding to Paris, demonstrating the cross-border appeal of social gaming venues. The concept blends sports simulation with food and beverage in a format increasingly popular with young adult audiences. FEC operators designing or refreshing venues for millennial and Gen Z guests should take note of this experience-plus-dining model.

articles.title: Morey's Piers Kicks Off 2026 Season with New Experiences and Major Renovations
articles.summary: Morey's Piers in Wildwood, New Jersey is launching its 2026 season with new attractions, a refurbished Giant Wheel, and improvements across the boardwalk. The seasonal amusement park's investment in renovation signals the importance of ongoing reinvention to drive repeat visitation. FEC operators can draw lessons from Morey's Piers approach to balancing new experiences with the upkeep of beloved legacy attractions.

articles.title: Kids Bowl Free Program Returns at Kentucky and Southern Indiana Locations This Summer
articles.summary: Several bowling centers in Kentucky and Southern Indiana are participating in the Kids Bowl Free summer program, offering free bowling to children and driving family traffic during the typically slower summer season. The program is a proven promotional tool for bowling operators to offset seasonal dips and introduce new customers to their facilities. FEC operators with bowling components should consider similar structured promotional programs to boost summer attendance.

articles.title: Former Grocery Store in Coldwater to Become Barcade and Market
articles.summary: A shuttered Family Fare grocery store in Coldwater, Michigan is being converted into a barcade and market concept, illustrating how vacant retail space is being creatively repurposed for entertainment use. The barcade format continues to grow as an adult-oriented entertainment option blending classic arcade gaming with craft beverage service. FEC suppliers and operators should monitor the barcade segment's expansion into underserved smaller markets.

articles.title: Sphere Abu Dhabi Receives $1.7 Billion Construction Contract, Opening Set for 2029
articles.summary: Abu Dhabi's culture and tourism department has awarded a $1.7 billion construction contract to Alec Engineering for Sphere Abu Dhabi on Yas Island, the first Sphere venue outside the US. The 20,000-capacity immersive entertainment venue will sit adjacent to existing theme parks and is expected to open by end of 2029. The project underscores the global push toward large-format immersive entertainment and the growing importance of Yas Island as an entertainment destination benchmark.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Indoor Entertainment Center in Lucknow, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone has expanded its entertainment network with a new location in Lucknow, reflecting the rapid growth of organized indoor entertainment in India's tier-2 cities. The opening highlights growing consumer demand for structured indoor family entertainment in emerging markets. International FEC suppliers and franchise operators should note India's accelerating FEC sector as a market entry opportunity.

articles.title: OKANA Resort Fully Opens for First Summer Season in Oklahoma City
articles.summary: OKANA Resort in Oklahoma City is now fully operational for its inaugural summer season, offering a waterpark, resort accommodations, and entertainment amenities. The property represents a new model of destination resort entertainment combining lodging with water-based and family attractions. FEC operators and investors should monitor OKANA's first-season performance as an indicator of demand for resort-integrated entertainment in non-coastal markets.

articles.title: Opinion: Theme Parks Should Be Transparent About Attendance Declines
articles.summary: An industry commentary argues that theme parks and attractions are not being forthcoming about the real causes of declining attendance, which may include pricing, value perception, and changing consumer behavior. For FEC operators, the piece raises important questions about how to communicate honestly with guests and investors during periods of soft performance. Operators should consider how pricing strategy and perceived value impact long-term loyalty in a competitive entertainment market.

articles.title: America 250 Wheel to Make Midwest Debut at Wisconsin State Fair
articles.summary: The America 250 Wheel, billed as the largest wheel to appear at Wisconsin State Fair Park, will make its Midwest debut at the fair's 175th anniversary celebration. Giant observation wheels remain a high-profile draw for seasonal and fair-based entertainment operators. Suppliers and operators in the portable and traveling attraction space should note the promotional value of record-breaking rides at major events.

articles.title: Rising Temperatures Cited as Key Driver of Indoor Entertainment Growth
articles.summary: An expert analysis from Nigeria points to escalating ambient temperatures as a primary catalyst for growth in indoor entertainment venues, a trend with global resonance. As outdoor leisure becomes less comfortable in many markets, enclosed FEC environments gain a structural advantage. Operators planning new locations or expanding should factor climate trends into site selection and marketing messaging.

articles.title: Balashi Beer Experience at Aruba's Gloria Entertainment Complex Reaches Installation Milestone
articles.summary: The Balashi Beer Experience, a 120-minute branded cultural attraction at Aruba's Gloria multi-entertainment complex, has reached on-site installation phase and is on track to open in late 2026. The project, developed by Sandenburg Experiences, caters to demand for indoor, air-conditioned attractions and is designed to extend guest dwell time at the complex. FEC operators can study this branded experiential model as an example of how anchor attractions drive ancillary spending within a mixed-use entertainment complex.

articles.title: Five Iron Golf Continues Middle East Expansion
articles.summary: Five Iron Golf, the indoor golf and entertainment brand, is gaining coverage in the Gulf region as it expands its international footprint. The brand's growth into Middle Eastern markets reflects rising global demand for active social entertainment concepts. FEC operators and investors in international markets should monitor Five Iron's expansion model as a benchmark for scaling experiential sports entertainment.

articles.title: Busch Gardens Parks Add Drone Shows and Bring Back Free Beer for Summer
articles.summary: Busch Gardens Tampa Bay and Williamsburg are adding new drone light shows featuring hundreds of synchronized drones as part of their summer event programming, along with returning free beer for adult guests. The additions illustrate how established parks are investing in spectacle-driven evening experiences to extend guest visits and boost per-cap spending. FEC operators running summer events should consider how drone shows and F&B promotions can increase dwell time and social media buzz.
